concerns.analysis: The output contains hallucinated AI thought process text injected directly into the body content on page 23 that does not exist in the source document.

concerns.analysis: Page 43 and Page 60 of the source PDF completely failed extraction and are entirely missing from the JSON output. The JSON content arrays for these pages are empty.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Page 43 text starting with: "(3) Each owner or operator subject to the provisions of this subpart who uses a boiler or process heater..."
Page 60 text starting with: "4. The Director shall notify the Permittee at least 60 days in advance..."
concerns.output_quote: The "content" array for page_number 43 and page_number 60 is empty: []

concerns.analysis: Several mathematical equations from the source PDF were incorrectly rendered as blank white images in the HTML, resulting in a loss of meaningful content. Specifically, the Q_m equation on page 12 and the A_co equation on page 50 were replaced with blank images.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Equation for Q_m on page 12 and equation for A_co on page 50.
concerns.output_quote: The equations are replaced with images carrying alt text such as "A completely blank, solid white rectangle." and "A completely blank white image."
concerns.location: Sections corresponding to page 12 and page 50

concerns.analysis: There are multiple instances of sub-list flattening throughout the document. Specifically, sub-items are often flattened into plain text within the parent list item's paragraph instead of being nested properly as list elements. For example, on page 11, the items (A), (B), and (C) under list item 'k. ii.' are merged into the text of 'ii.' rather than forming a sub-list. Additionally, on page 15, sub-items (1) and (2) under item '(F) Monitor and determine carbon monoxide concentrations' are incorr
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
ii. Operate the collection system with negative pressure at each wellhead except under the following conditions:
(A) A fire or increased well temperature...
(B) Use of a geomembrane or synthetic cover...
(C) A decommissioned well...

concerns.analysis: On page 19 of the JSON, the list items starting with 'i. Number of times that applicable parameters monitored...' through 'xi. Summary of all shut down wellheads...' are extracted once inside a child list, and then repeated identically immediately afterward as top-level list items. Furthermore, on page 31 of the JSON, the text block '(a) Except as provided in 40 CFR 62.16724(d)(2)...' along with its child elements is repeated seven times in a row. This constitutes a severe duplication of content
concerns.source_pdf_quote: i. Number of times that applicable parameters monitored under Section 2.1 A.3.k.ii, A.3.k.iii, and Section 2.1 A.3.k.iv above were exceeded...
concerns.output_quote: The entire sequence of list items i through xi is duplicated on page 19. Additionally, '(a) Except as provided in 40 CFR 62.16724(d)(2), the specified methods in paragraphs (a)(1) through (6)...' is repeated 7 times on page 31.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 2, Page 3, Page 5
Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 3 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis:
fallback_rendered_images=3. Examples: p1: "Logo for the North Carolina Department of Environm"; p12: "A completely blank, solid white rectangle."

5.  *"; p50: "A completely blank white image.". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 426 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=426, examples=['https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-40/part-62/subpart-OOO', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-40/section-62.16710', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-40/part-60']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (74): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; Permit No. 08849T09; Page 3
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=74, pages_affected=53/60. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.oah.nc.gov/hearings-division/filing/hearing-forms.; (https://www.epa.gov/electronic-reporting-air-emissions/compliance-and-emissions; (https://www3.epa.gov/ttn/chief/cedri/index.html).
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (3 instance(s)): p10→p11: ordered list J→K continues; p12→p13: list item ends “…ure gauge pressure in the gas collection”, continues as paragraph; p16→p17: ordered list X→Y continues
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=3. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 8 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. The monthly carbon monoxide emissions for the previous 12"; "a. changes in the information submitted in the application;"; "a. "Section 502(b)(10) changes" means changes that contraven"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=8. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Permittee:", "AOS", "Emission Source ID No."
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=3, no_th_tables=0. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description:
Incomplete image alt text: 8 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (8 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... (DEQ) featuring large green letters overlaid on a"; p5: "... Quality featuring a blue outline of the state map"; p12: "... rectangle."

5.  **Refine based on constraints:**"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=8 (truncated=8, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 426 link(s) clustered at bottom of 28 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=28, total_displaced=426. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p4(source:9cols,output:3cols), p9(source:9cols,output:3cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p4(source:9cols,output:3cols), p9(source:9cols,output:3cols)

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 7 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "This list is not intended to be a detailed record ". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
